audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Judges / Shoftim Bochim rebuke, Joshua death reprise, and judges-cycle theological introduction, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for angel of YHWH at Bochim, covenant rebuke, Israel's weeping, Joshua burial reprise, generational memory loss, Baal/Ashtaroth service, oppression, raised judges, relapse after judges' deaths, and nations left as testing.

verses.restored: Messenger of YHWH went up from Gilgal to Bochim and said, I brought you up from Egypt and brought you to land I swore to your fathers. I said, I will never break my covenant with you.
But you shall not cut covenant with inhabitants of this land; their altars you shall tear down. Yet you have not listened to my voice. What is this you have done?
So I also said, I will not drive them out from before you; they shall become sides/thorns for you, and their gods shall become a snare for you.
It was, when messenger of YHWH spoke these words to all children of Israel, that people lifted their voice and wept.
They called name of that place Bochim/Weepers, and they sacrificed there to YHWH.
Joshua sent people away, and children of Israel went each man to his inheritance to possess land.
People served YHWH all days of Joshua and all days of elders who lengthened days after Joshua, who had seen all great work of YHWH that he did for Israel.
Joshua son of Nun, servant of YHWH, died, one hundred ten years old.
They buried him in territory of his inheritance, at Timnath-heres, in hill country of Ephraim, north of Mount Gaash.
Also all that generation was gathered to its fathers, and another generation rose after them that did not know YHWH or the work he had done for Israel.
Children of Israel did evil in eyes of YHWH and served the Baals.
They forsook YHWH, Divine of their fathers, who brought them out from land of Egypt; they went after other gods, from gods of peoples around them, and bowed to them, and provoked YHWH.
They forsook YHWH and served Baal and the Ashtaroth.
Anger of YHWH burned against Israel, and he gave them into hand of plunderers who plundered them; he sold them into hand of enemies around them, and they could no longer stand before their enemies.
In all where they went out, hand of YHWH was against them for evil, as YHWH had spoken and as YHWH had sworn to them, and they were in great distress.
YHWH raised judges, and they saved them from hand of those plundering them.

Yet also to their judges they did not listen, for they prostituted themselves after other gods and bowed to them. They turned aside quickly from way their fathers walked, listening to commands of YHWH; they did not do so.

When YHWH raised judges for them, YHWH was with the judge and saved them from hand of their enemies all days of the judge, for YHWH was moved to pity by their groaning before those oppressing and pressing them.
It was, at death of judge, they returned and acted more corruptly than their fathers, going after other gods to serve them and bow to them. They did not drop from their deeds or stubborn way.
Anger of YHWH burned against Israel, and he said, Because this nation crossed my covenant that I commanded their fathers and did not listen to my voice,
I also will not continue to dispossess a man from before them, from nations Joshua left when he died,
in order to test Israel by them, whether they will keep way of YHWH to walk in it, as their fathers kept it, or not.
So YHWH left these nations, not dispossessing them quickly, and did not give them into hand of Joshua.

translation_notes: Bochim/Weepers names communal grief after covenant rebuke; tears and sacrifice do not automatically resolve the structural failure that follows.

The speech condemns covenants with land inhabitants and their altars within Israel's ancient covenant frame. It must not justify modern destruction of other religions' sacred sites or coercive anti-idolatry campaigns.
Judges reintroduces Joshua's death and burial with Timnath-heres, a variant from Joshua's Timnath-serah, requiring textual and place-name audit.
The 'generation that did not know' theme treats memory transmission as fragile. Forgetting is communal and educational, not proof that younger people are inherently worse.
Baal and Ashtaroth language belongs to Israel's internal covenant polemic and must not become demonization of modern religious minorities.
The judges cycle frames oppression through covenant theology: apostasy, distress, judge, rescue, relapse. It must not be used to blame victims of oppression or domestic violence for their suffering.
Sexual infidelity metaphors in verse 17 are religious rhetoric and must not be used for misogyny, sexual shame, or abuse of women.
safety_note: Do not use Judges 2 to justify religious persecution, destruction of sacred places, victim-blaming, anti-youth contempt, misogynistic sexual metaphors, or claims that oppressed communities caused their own abuse. Center memory transmission, Divine pity for groaning, and rescue from oppression without making suffering morally deserved.
